pain_points: Cold start problems - takes 20-40 viewing sessions to develop reasonable preference model, 18% new subscriber dissatisfaction
Fragmented data sources preventing unified preference modeling across 7 different systems with varying latency
No explainability in recommendations - 42% of users report lower trust due to black-box collaborative filtering
Binary recommendation logic ignores negative signals and context - users who skip content repeatedly still receive similar recommendations
Keyword-based search misses semantic intent and nuanced preferences - 34% of search results dismissed without engagement
No understanding of content attributes beyond surface-level metadata - cannot distinguish narrative complexity, tone, or perspective
Static content taxonomies from 2015 requiring manual updates with 3-5 day lag for new content
use_cases: Multi-dimensional preference understanding beyond genre matching using thematic similarity across content
Explainable recommendations with transparent reasoning and interactive feedback loops
Semantic content discovery with preference-aware ranking using vector embeddings and user preference vectors
Cold start personalization reducing time to accuracy from 20-40 sessions to 3-5 sessions
Negative preference learning to actively avoid recommending disliked content based on skip/dismissal patterns
customer_context.scale: 4.2M FIOS TV subscribers, 15M daily content interactions, 850M viewing events monthly, 200K on-demand titles, 425 live channels, 12K recommendation requests/second peak, 24TB Elasticsearch cluster
metrics: Engagement scores calculated from completion rate, rewind events, watch-to-end behavior
Time to personalization accuracy (current: 20-40 sessions, target: 3-5 sessions)
Semantic similarity scores for content matching
Preference alignment scores between user vectors and content embeddings
